title: Acceptability of a Dolutegravir Oral Dispersible Film in Term Neonates Born to Mothers Living With HIV.
abstract: Dolutegravir (DTG) is a key antiretroviral (ARV) drug for children living with HIV, but no specific formulation is available for neonates (<28 days old). One of the DTG formulations evaluated in the PETITE-DTG trial was a novel 5 mg oral DTG dispersible film (DTG-Film). Acceptability to end-users is essential when introducing new ARV drugs. We report on the experiences of mothers and health workers related to the DTG-Film.
trial_name: Integrated analysis of three phase I/II studies
